guest: Ryan Peterson, founder of Flexport and Import Genius

business_ideas.idea: Data-driven B2B shipping manifest platform for supply chain visibility
Building permit data search engine for contractor discovery
Premium phone booth designs for open office privacy
Water quality testing and accountability app using public data

predictions.text: Freight forwarding will be disrupted by direct-to-consumer logistics platforms
Phone booths will scale as open office necessity without proper infrastructure

dynamics: Peterson shares journey from bootstrapped Import Genius to VC-backed Flexport. Discussion covers finding overlooked opportunities, negotiation philosophy, and paradox of wealth. Sam asks about business models and decision-making.
frameworks: Schle blindness - blindness to biggest/most obvious problems in industries
Paradox of wealth - chasing money directly reduces your ability to make it
The six stakeholders model - customers, vendors, employees, investors, regulators, communities
Arbitrage through data access - public data + organization + distribution = business
Table selection - choosing which competition to engage matters more than skills
learnings: Biggest opportunities often in industries people have learned to accept dysfunction in
Focus on creating win-wins for all stakeholders rather than extracting maximum value
Personal values should guide business and life decisions consciously
Negotiations that feel like wins often create problems later with repeat counterparties
Cash flow beats net worth as measure of actual financial freedom

banger_quotes.quote: You can just do things - no permission needed if it's legal
The Paradox of wealth: focusing on money makes you make less money
Competing with knuckleheads is better than competing with people like you
Be expensive with your happiness, don't be cheap with your mood

numbers_dropped: Flexport: multi-billion dollar company (went through YC)
Import Genius: still profitable, millions in annual profit
Water taxi businesses: $400K per summer per boat, 3 boats = $1.2M annually
Phone booth company: reached $50M revenue before sale
E-commerce business: $50M+ revenue using Flexport and Import Genius
Charlie Munger real estate side hustle at age 90: $1B portfolio
notable: Unique perspective balancing bootstrap success (Import Genius still profitable) with VC growth (Flexport); emphasis on finding overlooked industries and long-term stakeholder value
